reviews.review_msg: This class has left me unprepared for my short practicum, and we did not cover anything of value that would help me develop as a teacher. The course was constantly changing last minute, with constant excuses. I view Cher as unhelpful, she said that things were often outside her control. Expectations were not well documented, and feedback unhelpful.

I question many of these ratings, as this is a pass/fail course. The course is subjective from top to bottom, and Cher ran the course in a very disorganized way. I still do not have a course outline from this course, and many of our weeks items were dropped on us the Thurs or Fri before, leaving us scrambling to do our readings and assignments.

I took Cher's class in my 1st year, she is very sweet. Her grading rubric is not very clear sometimes and she can be a bit disorganized with schedules, she changed the midterm due date a few days before the initial one last minute, then extended it to a day after the initial one. She assigned 300 word weekly, 1k word midterm, 4k word final.

Cher was an amazing instructor to have for EDUC 400. While there may have been problems with the program (PDP), none of these issues were attributable to Cher herself. Cher constantly went above and beyond for us, and I am so grateful to have worked with her. She is thoughtful, kind, intelligent, and an overall amazing person. Thank you Cher!

Cher was an amazing, kind, and considerate teacher who made EDUC 400 worthwhile. She built a strong class community that led to great memories and friendships. The EDUC 400 program felt disorganized at times, but Cher continuously worked to make the best of it. She listened to our feedback and adapted everything to support our comfort and wellbeing

Cher made the mundane topics of educ 400 fun, engaging and relevant. She is adaptive to her students needs, thoughtful, caring and the best educator who always shows up with her whole heart and open mind. Forever grateful and feeling lucky to have had her as my educ 400 prof ! Don't blame program disorganization on her !

So frustrating to see the negative review based on "disorganization" - the ONLY time there was anything that was disorganized had to do with the COURSE - the 300+ students, not OUR course with Cher. Having spoken to other students in the course who were not with Cher was really awaking, we were SOOO lucky to have Cher, she worked so hard for us!

Cher was an absolute pleasure of a prof to have for EDUC 400. She made every meeting together fun, engaging and educational. I have learnt a lot from her in this past semester and am looking forward to next sem! The person who left a bad review, is clearly unable to have an open mind. Cher is kind, down to earth and extremely educated!!!

EDUC 400 is a very drawn out course that does need a full semester however if you could have any teacher to help the semester go by faster it's CHER. Any negative reviews I feel are more towards the course material, not Cher's teaching. She is so kind, adapting to what WE need, encouraging, approachable, caring and just all around AWESOME !

Cher is an actual angel on earth. The most kind, caring, and genuine prof I've had over my years at SFU. I have never had someone care so much about student success. I was apprehensive going into the course, but she completely changed my mindset on education and learning. Will deeply miss her!

I think EDUC 400 is a waste of 4 months and $4000. Cher ran her class in a very disorganized way, often dropping things on us last minute. We had no meaningful outline as to what to expect during the course, and many of the assignments were quite pointless and did not improve my competency as a teacher candidate. I feel unprepared for EDUC 401.

The concept of EDUC 400, in full honesty, is kinda stupid to take up a whole semester with BUT if you have to go through it with anyone Cher's the best. She comes to the class with her whole self and has so much to give. She is very open to hearing what *we* want out of the class and adapting for it. Genuinely think any neg reviews are questionable

Cher's Exploration A is very disrespectful to Indigenous peoples, as it asks you to self-insert yourself like an activist rather than show respect. I will never understand why she encourages long, drawn-out land acknowledgements. Her encouragement of activism in class wasted a lot of time. Not easy to talk to if you are not an activist teacher.

EDUC 400 is a journey that asks you to think about the type of educator YOU want to be.  The profs can't do that work for you.  Thankfully, Cher is very approachable, caring, and supportive of her students.  She tries her best to connect with everyone in her classes on their level.  Trust the process, and if it isn't working for you... talk to her.

I took Education 400 with Cher this year; we did many of our activities Mundy Park. Many of the activities made no sense, like one time when we were painting rocks for a healing circle. This and many other activities did not help me develop my teaching skills. There is no schedule or syllabus for this course, which made it very difficult. I passed.

I sincerely think that she is an angel. She loves and inspires her students in depth. I never ever had this genuine professor in my life. My presentation in class changed my life and she supported me with lots of feedbacks. I hope SFU gives her many courses so many students can take her courses.
Nicest prof ever when I did this last spring. If she teaches again, take her.

Cher is an amazing prof. You have to work hard and put in the effort, but she definitely rewards the hard work. EXTREMELY student centered and will do what works best for her students, not what works best for her. I wish she taught more often! If you see her name on an outline, take the course--you won't regret it!

A very caring professor.  Cher really wants her students to get the most out of the class and expects full participation.  Do the readings, record your reflections after every class and offer your perspectives - this is a challening class in a great environment and will give you a strong foundation for PDP.
Great Prof ever!!!!! SO GOOD!!!She cares about all her students, she knows what she is teaching, she knows how to teach!!!! Great Prof!! I wish she can teach all my classes!!!!!

The most amazing proff I've ever had in my university career, I honestly wish she taught all my classes! She genuinely cares about all her students and is always looking for ways to self improve. i loved her!
This instructor knows her stuff. I've never worked so hard for a course, but she worked harder than any of us. LOTS of feedback. Very caring and community minded. Wish she taught all our courses!

Awesome prof! Wish she would teach more classes. Truly interested and concerned about her students. Knows her stuff and gives practical assignemnts. You will have fun while you learn! I'm doing a tech. educ. minor because of her. Thanks Cher!

TAKE THIS COURSE FROM CHER! My friends took with others, they were okay, but Cher is awesome! Her partner is great too - take any courses they teach. Both are caring, compassionate and competent instructors. I keep looking for them to teach more PDP courses! CM
Distance Ed course. Very helpful & quick to respond to ?'s. Tests are extremely hard, but assignments are easy. Great prof!
Simply the best. She should have more courses at SFU!
What can I say... She's better than those TA whom you see every week. You wouldn't believe that it's only a distance edu. course... SHE'S GREAT!
Best TM ever, 1 day max turn around on papers. Friendly and helpful.
